steps: Defined target persona: aspiring hawker / small F&B operator
Searched Reddit and public forums for location-selection pain points
Benchmarked pricing against feasibility consultants and NEA tools
Validated market size via NEA licensed establishment count on data.gov.sg
Designed 5-question survey instrument for WTP validation
survey_questions: Have you considered opening a hawker stall or coffeeshop in the next 12 months?
What is your biggest challenge when choosing a location? (rent / foot traffic / competition / unknown)
How much would you pay monthly for a tool that scores hawker centres by viability?
What data would you trust most: government licensing data, NEA rent benchmarks, or Google reviews?
Would you prefer a one-time location report ($49) or monthly subscription ($29)?

pain_points: Doesn't know how to compare AMR across centres
Afraid of over-bidding on NEA tender
interview_summaries.quote_theme: Location matters more than recipe for first-year survival

pain_points: Existing stall profitable but new location risky
Wants competition density by cuisine type
Consultant quotes too expensive for SME scale
interview_summaries.quote_theme: Paid consultant once for $5K; want cheaper ongoing intelligence

pain_points: Needs standardized scoring across 20+ candidate sites
Rental PSF varies 3x within same mall level
interview_summaries.quote_theme: Would pay for API access to batch-score locations

pricing_benchmarks.notes: Full feasibility study including footfall mapping and financial modeling
Financial calculator only; no location comparison or competition data

market_size.source_hawker_stalls: STAMPEDE hawker guide citing NEA statistics

wtp_estimate.rationale: 10-20x cheaper than one-time $3K-15K feasibility study; comparable to STAMPEDE POS pricing
